title: Lazy Peruvian Chicken Roast
description: A nutty, aerated, lazy-sunday Peruvian dish featuring balsamic vinegar and peanut butter for a high-protein, juicy pollo a la brasa style roast.
instructions: In a small bowl mix olive oil, balsamic vinegar, peanut butter, garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, oregano, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Place chicken thighs in a large baking dish and pour the mixture over them, ensuring each piece is coated.
Let the chicken marinate at room temperature for 15 minutes to absorb flavors.
Roast the chicken for 35-40 minutes, turning halfway, until skin is crispy and internal temperature reaches 165°F.
While the chicken is roasting, toast the almond slivers in a dry skillet over medium heat until golden, about 3-4 minutes.
Once the chicken is done, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Sprinkle the toasted almonds over the chicken, drizzle with any remaining juices from the pan, and serve hot.
